conversation_history.content: I'm building a web app with React and Node.js

{"response_type": "general", "analysis": "Okay, you're building a web application using React and Node.js. This is a common and relatively straightforward architecture. To begin understanding the potential security risks, I need more information about the specifics of your application.", "completeness_score": 0.2, "missing_info": ["What is the primary purpose of the web application?", "What type of data will the application process or store?", "What is the architecture of the Node.js backend (e.g., monolithic, microservices)?", "How will users authenticate (e.g., username/password, OAuth, etc.)?", "Where will the application be deployed (e.g., cloud provider, on-premise)?", "Are there any third-party libraries or services being used?"], "follow_up_questions": ["Could you describe the main components of the application \u2013 the frontend (React) and the backend (Node.js) \u2013 and how they interact?", "What kind of user data will be collected and stored?"], "ready_for_threat_model": false, "confidence_level": "low", "uncertainty_notes": ["My knowledge of the latest React and Node.js security best practices is limited without more specific details."], "world_model": {"system_type": "web application", "components": ["React frontend", "Node.js backend"], "data_types": [], "auth_method": "unknown", "network_exposure": "unknown", "compliance": [], "ai_involved": false}, "sources": [], "web_search_used": false, "reasoning_level": "balanced", "reasoning_summary": {"key_steps": [], "assumptions": [], "evidence_used": [], "confidence": "medium"}}

{"response_type": "threat_modeling", "analysis": "I understand you're having difficulty understanding the questions I'm asking.
